title: AMS encodes the envelope-altitude consensus as a Tier-2 principle
content: AMS encodes the envelope-altitude consensus as a Tier-2 principle: ams://canon/principles/envelope-altitude-consensus. The principle names the structural pattern observed across the 2025–2026 agent-comms protocol cluster (MCP, A2A, ACP, NLIP, AMP, ANP) — every serious entrant chose the envelope altitude (typed structure on the wire) — and contrasts it against AMS's choice of the token altitude (opaque chunks the wire never parses). The principle exists upstream of the planned resonance pages so each per-entrant comparison cites a shared divergence root rather than re-deriving it, per klappy://canon/principles/dry-canon-says-it-once. Tier 2 reflects that the principle is semi-stable (the cluster will grow; the load-bearing argument will not).

title: Six independent agent-comms protocols converged on the same wire altitude
content: Surveying the 2024–2026 wave of agent-comms protocols turns up at least six serious entrants — MCP (Anthropic, 2024), A2A (Google → Linux Foundation, April 2025), ACP (IBM/BeeAI → Linux Foundation, 2025), NLIP (Ecma TC56 → Ecma-430, December 2025), AMP (23blocks, 2026), and ANP — each shipping a different envelope (JSON-RPC, JSON-RPC + SSE, REST + MIME multipart, CBOR over WebSocket, signed Ed25519, DID-based) and each operating at the same altitude on the wire. RecursiveMAS (already encoded as resonance) sits one altitude below at latent vectors. AMS is the only entrant in the surveyed field at altitude 2 (opaque tokens). The convergence is independent: no entrant cites the others as influence, and the timeline is overlapping. Common pressure (discovery + auth + cross-org routing all want typed structure) explains it without coordination.

title: Batch comparison surfaces shared structure that per-entrant comparison cannot
content: The first comparison in this thread (AMS vs AMP) surfaced individual divergences along the wire-altitude axis. Pulling five more entrants through the same lens exposed a meta-pattern — envelope convergence — that no single comparison would have revealed. The operator's instinct to stay in exploration and survey the field before writing any resonance page was epistemically correct: batch comparison surfaces shared structure that is invisible from any single comparison. Encode-the-meta-first sequencing (the chosen path) means each subsequent resonance page becomes shorter and more pointed, because the load-bearing argument lives once and is referenced rather than re-derived. This is dry-canon-says-it-once applied to the resonance convention.

title: Per-entrant resonance pages must add entrant-specific divergence beyond the shared root
content: A resonance page that cites only ams://canon/principles/envelope-altitude-consensus and adds no entrant-specific detail has not done the work. The shared divergence is the root; per-entrant divergences are the leaves. Each per-entrant resonance page (under canon/resonance/) must (a) cite the principle for the shared envelope-vs-token divergence, (b) name the entrant's envelope specifically — what fields, what serialization, what the envelope commits the protocol to, (c) surface at least one entrant-specific divergence beyond the shared one (e.g. SSE vs WebSocket for A2A; DIDs and RBAC for ACP; natural language as substrate for NLIP; mandatory Ed25519 for AMP; tool-call semantics vs subscriber semantics for MCP), (d) honor the divergence-mandatory rule from klappy://canon/resonance. Naming this constraint up front prevents thin "see envelope-altitude-consensus" pointer pages and keeps the resonance convention's epistemic value intact.

title: canon/principles/ has no README index either
content: Per the recursive-mas-resonance entry's "MD indexes are a lie in wait" observation, canon/resonance/ deliberately ships without a README index. canon/principles/ also has no index. The new envelope-altitude-consensus principle adds itself as a sibling in the same un-indexed pattern. Open question for a future session: whether the no-README-index stance generalizes across all canon/ subdirectories as a hard convention, or remains a per-directory choice. Discoverability lives in oddkit_search and cross-reference rather than in static enumeration.

title: Next: write canon/resonance/agent-messaging-protocol.md and canon/resonance/a2a.md as the first batch
content: Next planned scope returns to planning, then execution: write the first two per-entrant resonance pages using ams://canon/principles/envelope-altitude-consensus as anchor. AMP (canon/resonance/agent-messaging-protocol.md) is the lower-effort start because the comparison was largely drafted in-thread already and just needs to be reshaped into the canon/resonance/ template (8 fixed sections, file-named-after-the-work). A2A (canon/resonance/a2a.md) is the higher-leverage start because it is the industry consensus standard (AWS/Cisco/Google/Microsoft/Salesforce/SAP/ServiceNow backing) and AMS most needs legibility against it. Recommended order: AMP first as the warm-up, then A2A and MCP, then ACP and NLIP. ANP gets a separate read-and-screen pass before promotion. The orchestration-frameworks cluster (LangGraph/AutoGen/CrewAI) is screened as a single class-page, not per-entrant.

title: canon/principles/envelope-altitude-consensus.md committed
content: canon/principles/envelope-altitude-consensus.md committed. URI: ams://canon/principles/envelope-altitude-consensus. Tier 2. Frontmatter complete (uri, title, audience=canon, exposure=nav, tier=2, voice=neutral, stability=semi_stable, tags, epoch=E0008.4, date=2026-05-02, derives_from, complements, governs, status=active). Section structure matches the existing canon principle shape (operator-as-subscriber, vodka-architecture-applied): pull-quote intro, Description, Outline, named H2 sections, "What This Is Not", "See Also". Voice-cliche audited against klappy://canon/constraints/ai-voice-cliches: no formulaic transitions, no summary clichés, no sycophantic openers, no negation parallelism as paragraph closer, no puffing importance, no generic positive descriptors, em-dash density within bounds (one borderline in a markdown table; rewritten with colons to clear the threshold). Cross-references: ams://canon/constraints/permanent-non-goals (the non-goals list this principle puts in context), ams://canon/principles/vodka-architecture-applied (the discipline that produces the altitude choice), ams://canon/decisions/D0001-tokens-not-messages (the irreversible wire-altitude decision this principle defends), ams://canon/decisions/D0009-stream-as-primitive-ownership-excludes-subscription (the load-bearing pub-sub rule), ams://canon/resonance/recursive-mas (the latent-altitude alternative; same divergence pattern, opposite direction), AMS.md §3.1 and §11, klappy://canon/resonance (the convention this principle anchors), klappy://canon/principles/dry-canon-says-it-once (why the principle exists upstream of the resonance pages).
